title: Bad parsing of `DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS` in v3.0
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

In `vendor/github.com/containers/common/pkg/config/config.go`, the value of environment variable `DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS` is parsed.  In v3.0 branch, this parsing is not 100% correct.

The environment variable's value appears to be a comma-separated list of key-value pairs, although I haven't found a definitive document that specifies the format of this environment variable: the DBUS specification seems rather vague on this point.

In the v3.0 branch, the code assumes the environment variable contains no comma.  The code correctly parses the environment variable's value if it contains a list with a single item.

Commit #732ece6ae2 fixes this problem by introducing new code (in `config.go`) that splits the environment variable's value on commas, parsing each list item in turn.  Unfortunately, this fix is only available in podman v3.3.0 (and later).

```
paul@celebrimbor:~/git/podman (main)$ git tag --contains 732ece6ae2
v3.3.0
[...]
paul@celebrimbor:~/git/podman (main)$ 
```

It would be helpful if this fix for parsing `DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS` values were to be back-ported to earlier branches.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Run `podman ps` on an up-to-date Debian "bullseye" machine.

**Describe the results you received:**

```
paul@celebrimbor:~/git/podman (main)$ podman ps 
WARN[0000] The cgroupv2 manager is set to systemd but there is no systemd user session available 
WARN[0000] For using systemd, you may need to login using an user session 
WARN[0000] Alternatively, you can enable lingering with: `loginctl enable-linger 1000` (possibly as root) 
WARN[0000] Falling back to --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs    
WARN[0000] The cgroupv2 manager is set to systemd but there is no systemd user session available 
WARN[0000] For using systemd, you may need to login using an user session 
WARN[0000] Alternatively, you can enable lingering with: `loginctl enable-linger 1000` (possibly as root) 
WARN[0000] Falling back to --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs    
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE   COMMAND  CREATED  STATUS  PORTS   NAMES
paul@celebrimbor:~/git/podman (main)$ 
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

The same output, but without the warning:

```
paul@celebrimbor:~/git/podman (main)$ podman ps
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE   COMMAND  CREATED  STATUS  PORTS   NAMES
paul@celebrimbor:~/git/podman (main)$ 
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

There is a relatively simple work-around by reformatting the `DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS` environment variable in the format that the older version supports; e.g.,

```
paul@celebrimbor:~/git/podman (main)$ echo $DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS
unix:path=/run/user/1000/bus,guid=5c5c86e60aa45c4c51dcfa0a630db85e
paul@celebrimbor:~/git/podman (main)$ DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS=$(echo $DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS | cut -d, -f1) podman ps
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE   COMMAND  CREATED  STATUS  PORTS   NAMES
paul@celebrimbor:~/git/podman (main)$ 
```
(This assumes that the `unix:path` item is the first element in the list.)

To me, this suggests the underlying problem is the `DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS` parsing bug, which was addressed in commit #732ece6ae2.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
paul@celebrimbor:~/git/podman (main)$ podman -v
podman version 3.0.1
paul@celebrimbor:~/git/podman (main)$ 
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

```
paul@celebrimbor:~/git/podman (main)$ podman info
WARN[0000] The cgroupv2 manager is set to systemd but there is no systemd user session available 
WARN[0000] For using systemd, you may need to login using an user session 
WARN[0000] Alternatively, you can enable lingering with: `loginctl enable-linger 1000` (possibly as root) 
WARN[0000] Falling back to --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs    
WARN[0000] The cgroupv2 manager is set to systemd but there is no systemd user session available 
WARN[0000] For using systemd, you may need to login using an user session 
WARN[0000] Alternatively, you can enable lingering with: `loginctl enable-linger 1000` (possibly as root) 
WARN[0000] Falling back to --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
paul@celebrimbor:~/git/podman (main)$ apt list podman
Listing... Done
podman/stable,now 3.0.1+dfsg1-3+deb11u1 amd64 [installed]
podman/stable 3.0.1+dfsg1-3+deb11u1 i386
paul@celebrimbor:~/git/podman (main)$ 
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes.

This problem does not appear to be listed in the troubleshooting document.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out, @paulmillar!

Since the issue affects downstream Debian, could you open an issue on the Debian Bugzilla?

Cc: @siretart
Please file a bug with debian, and please do add a link to this conversation there. We then can investigate what further steps are appropriate
